g.onclick: sendPrompt('When is a full depth snapshot sent and what does it contain?')
sendPrompt('What does the internal book replica look like after receiving the full snapshot?')
sendPrompt('What does an incremental depth update look like?')
sendPrompt('What happens when a subscriber detects a sequence gap in the feed?')
sendPrompt('How does a subscriber recover from a sequence gap?')

svg.text: Incremental is the steady state — most messages are tiny (1 level changed). Efficient at scale.
Full snapshot is expensive — sends everything. Only on connect or after a gap. Never in steady state.
Sequence numbers make the system self-healing — any gap is detectable and recoverable.
A subscriber that missed seq=4 and applied seq=5,6 has a silently wrong book — worse than crashing.
This is why gap detection + snapshot recovery is not optional. Ghost orders and invisible orders
corrupt every strategy decision silently until you figure out why your fills don't match expectations.
